subject: UX & Research
title: The $300 Million Button
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The $300 Million Button.

hook.text: An e-commerce site required account creation before checkout. One UX change added $300 million in revenue.

buildup.text: Jared Spool found that 45% of users had multiple accounts they'd forgotten. Registration was a brick wall.

discovery.text: The fix: replace the 'Register' button with 'Continue as Guest.' Let people buy first, register later if they wanted.

twist.text: Sales jumped $300 million in the first year. Same products, same prices. Just one less step.

climax.text: Forced registration before purchase adds friction at the worst moment — when users are ready to pay.

punchline.text: Remove friction at checkout. One button change made millions.

quiz.question: What change generated $300 million in additional revenue?
options: Replacing forced registration with a guest checkout option
